plan: Queue carry as primary. It is your best role at 54.4% over 168 games (your overall is 51.5%). Set midlane as secondary, not the other way around.
Stop queueing midlane: 46.0% over 200 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play your safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Your champion pool is 10+ heroes wide and your top three are only 36% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
The math: you are at 653 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 824.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: Aurora is not just your best hero, it is your most disciplined
insights.finding: On Aurora you die 2.8/game vs your 4.0 average. The winrate edge is partly a discipline edge; that habit can travel to other picks.
insights.receipt: 86 games on Aurora, 51.9% shrunk vs field 50.1%

insights.title: Goldilocks324 brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Goldilocks324 is 56% over 202 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 2 pentakills in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 21 (plus 18 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Cuban has receipts.

insights.title: Your lean, relative to the squad: objective damage
insights.finding: Cuban's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is objective damage at 1.1x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.

coachReasoning.assessment: Your ceiling is loud — a 16/1/1 Sparrow carry game with just 1 death shows what you look like at your best — but your recent losses came off-carry and over-extended: 6 deaths on Zinx mid (4/6/11) and 10 on Khaimera jungle (3/10/2). The single biggest lever is bringing Aurora's discipline to every game and leaning on your carry picks where you are genuinely strong.
plan: On Sparrow, replay your 16/1/1 game as the template — hit from max range behind your team, use your dash purely to reset spacing, and let your positioning (not aggression) rack up the kills; that 1-death discipline is your win condition.
On Aurora, keep doing what makes her your most disciplined hero — you die just 2.8 per game on her — and carry that same patience to Sparrow and Serath so your death spikes stop.
On Serath, your best hero at 59.6% over 94 games, use your flight ultimate to escape or reposition after a dive rather than to greed a kill — commit only when you can burst a target and get out.
On Zinx, the 4/6/11 mid loss was a death problem — poke from range and wait for your ultimate to land on a grouped enemy instead of walking up; but this is a role you can safely deprioritize.

Stop bleeding on picks you die on: your recent 6- and 10-death games came off your comfort carries — when you land carry, default to Sparrow or Serath and play the patient, back-line game that already wins for you.

insights.title: Discipline is your real edge
insights.finding: On Aurora you die 2.8 per game versus your 4.0 average, and your best Sparrow game was 16/1/1 with 1 death — the winrate follows the low deaths; make that spacing your default.

insights.title: Sparrow and Serath are your weapons
insights.finding: 68.1% over 72 games on Sparrow and 59.6% over 94 on Serath are your standout picks; leaning into them beats spreading across a 10-hero pool.

insights.title: Death spikes sank the losses
insights.finding: Your losses were the games you over-committed — 6 deaths on Zinx, 10 on Khaimera versus a 4-death norm; the patient Sparrow game shows you already know the fix.

insights.title: Countess is the drag
insights.finding: 37.9% over 87 games on Countess pulls hard against your strong carries; benching her for Sparrow/Serath is free rating.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
